decision_claim: Runtime self-evolution must be evidence-triggered, source-prioritized, default dry-run, and bounded by the existing build -> test -> contract/invariant -> hotspot verification floor.

automatic_trigger.cadence: at least every 30 days after last successful evolution review

source_priority.use: authoritative semantics for Codex, Claude Code, GitHub Actions, PowerShell, Python, dependency managers, and owned runtime APIs
implementation pattern comparison; never copied without repo fit and gate evidence
target-run failures, host feedback, doctor findings, claim drift, repeated manual work, and target repo rollout data
agent-session lessons, learning-efficiency metrics, controlled improvement proposals, knowledge-source curation, and skill-manifest candidates from real AI coding work
candidate signal only; cannot override official docs, local code facts, or repository policy

source_catalog.summary: OpenAI Codex AGENTS.md loading and project instruction semantics.
OpenAI Agents SDK evolution guidance around eval-driven agent iteration, builder workflows, and optimization loops.
OpenAI hosted sandbox guidance for agent execution isolation and environment control.
OpenAI guardrail and approval guidance for risk-aware human review in agent workflows.
OpenAI Codex subagent concept surface for delegated parallel work while preserving reviewable parent control.
OpenAI Codex sandbox and approval-mode semantics for local execution boundaries.
Claude Code hierarchical settings model and project/user/managed configuration precedence.
Claude Code hook lifecycle for deterministic shell-command controls around agent tool use.
Gemini CLI settings precedence, project configuration, and host-level operator controls.
Gemini CLI plan-mode semantics for read-only planning before implementation.
Gemini CLI MCP server support across stdio, SSE, and streamable HTTP transports.
MCP security best practices for consent, control, tool safety, and prompt-injection boundaries.
GitHub Actions schedule semantics, limitations, and workflow_dispatch relationship.
Primary Codex project surface for release and instruction-loading drift signals.
OpenHands runtime documentation as a reference implementation signal for sandboxed agent execution.
Aider repo map as a reference pattern for compact repository context shaping.
SWE-agent agent-computer-interface documentation as a reference signal for inspect/edit/test workflows.
Reference practice for deciding what repeated manual work should become automation.
Local contract for turning AI coding interaction friction, clarification, and handoff signals into reviewable metrics.
Local proposal contract for converting recurring AI coding lessons into governed policy, hook, skill, control, knowledge, or repo follow-up candidates.
Local skill manifest contract for promoting repeated coding know-how into reusable, reviewable skills.

add_or_modify_criteria: official source changes make the current local assumption stale, unsupported, or less safe
internal evidence shows repeated failures, repeated manual toil, or high-friction operator work
AI coding sessions repeatedly surface the same decision, repair pattern, prompt pattern, or tool workflow and it can be skillized with evidence and gates
a small bounded change improves verification, evidence quality, rollback, security, or cross-repo consistency
the change preserves the runtime identity as governance sidecar / attach-first runtime rather than host replacement
delete_or_retire_criteria: the capability has no recent usage evidence and its maintenance cost is visible
a narrower or existing canonical entrypoint already covers the behavior
the feature is narrative-only and lacks tests, contract, evidence, or operator path
the feature weakens security, provenance, compatibility, or the host-neutral governance boundary
the feature is a heavy-stack candidate without a selected LTP scope fence

risk_automation_boundaries.low: may auto-generate docs, candidate reports, evidence refreshes, and dry-run patches after gates pass
risk_automation_boundaries.medium: may generate a patch plan and evidence, but must not apply rules, profiles, baselines, or target repo sync without explicit scope
risk_automation_boundaries.high: must stop at scope fence, rollback plan, and full-gate evidence; no automatic apply

invariants: Evolution review is not permission to self-mutate without evidence.
Official documentation and local running facts outrank community examples.
AI coding experience can propose skill, knowledge, hook, policy, or control changes, but it must remain reviewable evidence rather than hidden memory authority.
The first implementation phase must be dry-run by default.
Every accepted candidate must preserve source -> candidate -> decision -> patch -> gate -> evidence -> rollback traceability.
No heavy LTP package may start unless the existing LTP promotion policy selects exactly one package or the user explicitly labels owner-directed scope.

scheduled_check.purpose: Run dry-run evolution review and Docs freshness gate on a schedule and on workflow_dispatch.

rollback_ref: git revert the runtime evolution policy, implementation plan, backlog tasks, evidence, and later scripts or gates introduced by GAP-120..124
